confidence_note: Scores and density bands are pre-validation research estimates and must be confirmed with a 30-lead mini-validation scrape before outreach.

summary.most_missed_setting: outer-ring suburban service-area operators in fast-growth Sun Belt metros

why_it_fits: hot-climate recurring service economics
clear service-area model
good phone/email publish rate
easy template fit for offers, pricing, testimonials, and booking
visible website weakness in owner-operator segment
before-and-after proof is easy to sell
storm and removal demand supports strong lead value
good fit for direct-response local landing pages

top_5.growth_signal: Raleigh-Cary grew 2.4% from 2024 to 2025 and ranked top 10 U.S. metros by percent growth
why_it_fits: high-intent emergency and repair demand
simple CTA structure
strong suburban housing footprint
good fit for fast demo generation

top_5.growth_signal: Jacksonville city added 16,365 residents in the 2023-2024 city release
why_it_fits: weather-exposed service demand
fragmented local operator base
strong photo and trust-signal upgrade opportunity
good balance of density and manageable competition
large recurring-service market
lots of service pages that can be standardized
healthy density without immediately forcing the hardest agency fights
strong fit for managed monthly updates

high_confidence_shortlist.note: Great niche economics and density; more competitive than Phoenix.
Outer-edge DFW growth supports suburban service-area operators.
Large and still growing; solid owner-operator opportunity.
Climate fit is excellent; medium competition.
Useful non-Sun-Belt control market with direct-response demand.

expansion_watchlist.note: Fastest-growing metro by percent growth; strong visual-service fit.
Fast-growth coastal metro with weather-driven service need.
Strong growth and recurring-service logic; watch franchise density.
Fast-growth suburban housing profile suits direct-response service pages.
Lower elite-agency pressure than major metros; worth mini-validating.
Strong growth and pool economics; seasonality pushes it later.

missing_niches_or_settings.name: pressure washing
missing_niches_or_settings.reason: Visual before-and-after niche with owner-operator density and easy repeatable templates.
missing_niches_or_settings.market_signal: IBISWorld reported 32,193 U.S. pressure washing businesses in 2024, up 5.2% year over year.

missing_niches_or_settings.name: appliance repair
missing_niches_or_settings.reason: Service-area and urgent-intent niche with straightforward CTA structure.
missing_niches_or_settings.market_signal: IBISWorld reported 37,104 U.S. appliance repair businesses in 2025 and 37,453 in 2026.

missing_niches_or_settings.name: selective pest control
missing_niches_or_settings.reason: Recurring-service niche with strong contactability and local conversion logic, but avoid franchise-heavy markets.
missing_niches_or_settings.market_signal: IBISWorld reported 33,197 U.S. pest-control businesses in 2025 and 34,076 in 2026.

missing_niches_or_settings.name: outer-ring suburban counties in fast-growth metros
missing_niches_or_settings.reason: The latest Census release says the fastest-growing counties around large metros tended to be on the outer edges, especially in Texas.

missing_niches_or_settings.name: review-strong but site-weak businesses
missing_niches_or_settings.reason: Review-first behavior means the website is often the second trust check after Maps and reviews, not the first discovery asset.

material_trends.trend: AI builders now sell AI discoverability, not just site generation
material_trends.signal: Durable now advertises Advanced GEO and weekly AI visibility ranking.
material_trends.impact: Generic cheap website positioning becomes less defensible.

material_trends.trend: Review-first and maps-first local behavior is strengthening
material_trends.signal: BrightLocal 2026 reported 41% always read reviews and 54% visit the website after positive reviews.
material_trends.impact: Site pitch must tie into reviews, trust, and conversion, not pages alone.

material_trends.trend: Official Google Places products are more usable for scoring workflows
material_trends.signal: Places Aggregate API now supports counts and place IDs, while Places API New uses field-mask billing and Text Search caps at 60 results.
material_trends.impact: Plan a phased migration from scraping to official APIs for pair scoring and scale.

material_trends.trend: Growth is concentrating in Southeast and Sun Belt outer edges
material_trends.signal: Census 2026 said many of the fastest-growing counties were along the Southeast coast and on outer metro edges.
material_trends.impact: Fast-growth suburban metros should be prioritized over prestige urban cores.

material_trends.trend: Small businesses are already in redesign and update mode
material_trends.signal: Clutch 2026 reported 61% update weekly, 81% have redesigned before, and 90% plan site investment.
material_trends.impact: Recurring managed updates are a better pitch than one-time 'we'll build you a site' language.
